methodology: Articles sourced from Virginia-specific RSS feeds and Google News RSS, filtered for AI/technology/workforce relevance, then assessed by Claude against the scenario framework. Each article is tagged with the region it most affects and the scenario it signals toward.

signals.title: AI pressure grows on Richmond's young workforce - Axios
signals.signal_reading: AI adoption pressure on Richmond's young workforce signals deepening capability concentration in metro areas where AI reshapes job requirements faster than education systems can adapt, potentially widening the skills gap between hub and periphery regions.

signals.title: Virginia’s AI Data Center Gravy Train Shows Signs of Slowing - Bloomberg Law News
signals.signal_reading: Slowing data center expansion in NoVA represents infrastructure concentration reaching saturation rather than distribution, reinforcing that AI capability buildout follows existing tech corridors rather than spreading to underserved regions.

signals.title: EPA grant backs community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia - Virginia Tech News
signals.signal_reading: EPA-funded community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia signals potential for distributed capacity building through local institutions, though success depends on whether training creates deep AI fluency or shallow platform skills.

signals.title: New partnership between VCU and AI Ready RVA aims to boost AI education, innovation and community impact - VCU News
signals.signal_reading: VCU-AI Ready RVA partnership aims to build distributed AI education and community impact infrastructure, representing institutional commitment to deep skill development beyond elite tech corridors, though scalability beyond Richmond remains uncertain.

signals.title: Hampton Roads multiple listing service introduces AI tool to personalize home searches - The Virginian-Pilot
signals.signal_reading: Hampton Roads MLS deploying consumer-facing AI search tools exemplifies shallow, platform-delivered adoption—users gain AI benefits without building underlying capability, creating broad but surface-level distribution that doesn't develop regional expertise.
